title: DeepMind Founded
description: Demis Hassabis, Shane Legg, and Mustafa Suleyman founded DeepMind Technologies in London, UK. The company's mission is to 'solve intelligence, and then use intelligence to solve everything else'.

title: Google Acquires DeepMind
description: Google acquired DeepMind for approximately £400 million to £650 million, one of Google's largest European acquisitions at the time. DeepMind became an independently operated AI research lab under Google.

title: AlphaGo First Defeats Professional Go Player
description: DeepMind's AlphaGo defeated European Go champion Fan Hui 5:0, becoming the first computer program to defeat a professional Go player. This achievement was published in the journal Nature.

title: AlphaGo Defeats Lee Sedol
description: AlphaGo defeated world Go champion Lee Sedol 9-dan 4:1 in Seoul, South Korea. The match was watched by over 280 million people globally, marking a historic moment in AI development.

title: Google Brain Launches TensorFlow
description: Google Brain team open-sourced the machine learning framework TensorFlow 1.0, which quickly became one of the world's most popular deep learning frameworks, reshaping the AI development ecosystem.

title: Transformer Paper Published
description: Google Brain team published 'Attention Is All You Need', proposing the Transformer architecture. This paper completely transformed the natural language processing field, becoming the foundation for modern large language models such as GPT and BERT.

title: DeepMind Launches AlphaGo Zero
description: AlphaGo Zero learned solely through self-play without any human game records, surpassing the AlphaGo version that defeated Lee Sedol within three days. Demonstrated the powerful potential of reinforcement learning.

title: Google Duplex Stunning Debut
description: Google Duplex AI demonstrated at Google I/O conference could make natural voice calls to book restaurants and hair appointments. Its realism sparked widespread discussion about AI ethics and transparency.

title: BERT Model Released
description: Google released BERT (Bidirectional Encoder Representations from Transformers), which understands context through bidirectional training, setting new records on 11 NLP tasks.

title: AlphaFold2 Cracks Protein Folding Problem
description: DeepMind's AlphaFold2 achieved atomic-level accuracy in predicting protein structures at the CASP14 competition, regarded as solving a major challenge that had plagued biology for 50 years. Results published in Nature.

title: AlphaFold Protein Database Made Public
description: DeepMind collaborated with EMBL-EBI to make public over 350,000 protein structure predictions, covering nearly all proteins in the human genome. This resource is freely available to global researchers.

title: Google Publishes PaLM Paper
description: Published Pathways Language Model (PaLM), a 540B parameter large language model demonstrating breakthrough few-shot learning capabilities on multiple reasoning tasks.

title: Google Brain Launches PaLM
description: Google Brain officially released PaLM, demonstrating its powerful capabilities in commonsense reasoning, mathematics, and code generation, regarded as Google's important response to GPT-3.

title: LaMDA Dialogue Model Sparks 'Consciousness' Controversy
description: Google engineer Blake Lemoine claimed LaMDA had gained self-consciousness, sparking global debate about AI consciousness and ethics. Google denied the claim and suspended him.

title: Google Releases Bard
description: Facing competitive pressure from ChatGPT, Google urgently released conversational AI Bard. A factual error during the first demonstration caused Google's stock price to drop over $100 billion in a single day.

title: Bard Opens Public Beta
description: Bard opened waitlist testing in the US and UK, based on a lightweight LaMDA version. Google emphasized this was an 'experimental' product, attempting to balance innovation and safety.

title: Google Brain and DeepMind Merge
description: Google announced the merger of Google Brain and DeepMind into Google DeepMind, with Demis Hassabis as CEO. This integration aimed to concentrate resources to compete with OpenAI.

title: Google I/O Releases PaLM 2
description: PaLM 2 was released at Google I/O conference, supporting multilingual and code generation capabilities, and providing underlying support for Bard. Multiple size versions were launched to adapt to different application scenarios.

title: Gemini 1.0 Officially Released
description: Google released the native multimodal large model Gemini 1.0, including three versions: Ultra, Pro, and Nano. Google claimed Ultra surpassed GPT-4 on 30 of 32 benchmarks.

title: Bard Renamed to Gemini
description: Google fully renamed Bard to Gemini and launched the Gemini Advanced subscription service ($20/month), powered by the Gemini Ultra model. Marks the repositioning of Google's AI product strategy.

title: Google I/O Releases Gemini 1.5 Pro
description: Released Gemini 1.5 Pro, introducing a breakthrough 1 million token context window (later expanded to 2 million), and demonstrating astonishing 'needle in a haystack' long-context retrieval capabilities.

title: Google Releases Gemini 1.5 Flash
description: Launched Gemini 1.5 Flash, optimized for speed and efficiency, significantly reducing latency and cost while maintaining strong performance, forming a high-low pairing with Pro.

title: AlphaProteo Released
description: DeepMind released AlphaProteo, an AI system specifically designed for novel protein binders, performing significantly better than existing best methods across 7 target protein tests.

title: Gemini 2.0 Flash Experimental Released
description: Google released Gemini 2.0 Flash Experimental, emphasizing native multimodal output (images, audio) and agentic capabilities (Agentic AI), laying the foundation for next-generation AI assistants.

title: Gemini 2.0 Series Fully Launched
description: Gemini 2.0 Flash, Pro, and other models fully launched, supporting multimodal understanding and generation of text, images, audio, and video. Google emphasized its 'agentic AI' vision.

title: Gemini 2.5 Pro Released
description: Google released Gemini 2.5 Pro, dubbed the 'most powerful model', with outstanding performance in code, mathematics, and scientific reasoning, regarded as Google's direct response to GPT-4.5 and Claude 3.7.

title: Google Brain Project Launched
description: Google internally launched the Google Brain project, led by Andrew Ng, Jeff Dean, and Greg Corrado, focusing on deep learning research. The project initially used 16,000 CPU cores to train neural networks.

title: Google Brain Cat Image Recognition Breakthrough
description: Google Brain's neural network trained on 10 million YouTube video screenshots spontaneously learned to recognize cat faces. After publication, this result triggered a deep learning boom and is regarded as one of the starting points of the modern AI wave.

title: Google Declares Quantum Supremacy
description: Google published a paper in the journal Nature, announcing that its 53-qubit processor Sycamore achieved 'quantum supremacy' on a specific task, completing in 200 seconds what would take a traditional supercomputer 10,000 years.

title: LaMDA Released
description: Google released LaMDA (Language Model for Dialogue Applications), a 137B parameter model specifically designed for dialogue, emphasizing factuality and safety.
